pr.title: More dups and a ft link
pr.author: lukechampine

summary: Continuation of #2572 that linked two Kirby capture fighter units and modeled more duplicate/adjacent stage data strings. The PR marked ftCo_CaptureKirby.c and ftCo_CaptureWaitKirby.c as Matching, split their .sdata ownership in GALE01 splits, converted many anonymous/global data symbols to local @ symbols, and added StageData wrapper structs plus explicit OSReport filename/format strings across several gr stage files. decomp-dev reported 18 new matches, +4304 matched code bytes, +2116 linked code bytes, and small matched/linked data gains.

key_files.role: Changed ftCo_CaptureKirby.c and ftCo_CaptureWaitKirby.c from NonMatching to Matching.
Split the Kirby capture .sdata ranges: CaptureKirby ends at 0x804D3BC8, CaptureWaitKirby owns 0x804D3BC8-0x804D3BE0, and ThrownKirby owns 0x804D3BE0-0x804D3BF8.
Reclassified many data, sdata, and sdata2 objects from lbl_/ftCo_ globals to local @ symbols; added local ...data.0 and split grCs_803E11A4 trailing strings.
Main fighter match work for ftCo_800BD1DC and ftCo_800BD39C: include cleanup, inline reshaping, PAD_STACK, stack-local layout tricks, and removal of fake const literals.
Removed extern declarations for ftCo_804D3BE0/BE8/BF0 after .sdata ownership moved to local symbols.
Used explicit u8 stack pads and PAD_STACK sizes to finish two Yoshi-copy Kirby capture animation matches.
Materialized grKg_803E16E0, grKg_803E1704, grKg_803E1800, report format, and filename data; fixed callback indexing and OSReport data references to match grKongo_801D5340.
Changed a placeholder static StageCallbacks pointer into exact extern S16Vec3 and StageCallbacks arrays.
Added grBb_StageData with trailing report_format and changed OSReport to use data-offset pointers.
Added data padding, grMc_StageData, explicit filename/report strings, baselib/debug include, and __assert calls for existing spline checks.
Added data padding plus grIm_StageData with report/assert strings and adjusted OSReport address usage.
Restructured grVenom_80205AD4 and grVenom_80206874 with gotos, explicit zero stores, pointer casts, and bitfield layout casts to raise them near-match.
Made callback arrays static const and separated /GrCs.dat into a named char array used by StageData.
Added explicit report padding, OSReport format string, and filename string to improve data/code references.
Minor local declaration order change in it_8026EECC_inline_1.

what_changed: The build configuration now treats melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_CaptureKirby.c and ftCo_CaptureWaitKirby.c as Matching objects instead of NonMatching.
GALE01 splits were corrected so CaptureKirby, CaptureWaitKirby, and ThrownKirby each own their proper .sdata subrange rather than CaptureKirby owning 0x804D3BB0-0x804D3BF8 as one block.

GALE01 symbols were adjusted from broad lbl_/ftCo_ globals to local @ symbols for several jump tables, strings, floats, and doubles, including ftCo_804D3BB0 through ftCo_804D3BF0 and ftCo_804D8BA8 through ftCo_804D8BC8.

ftCo_CaptureKirby.c was cleaned up and reshaped for matching: placeholder/platform and unused includes were removed, fake literal constants were removed, inlineA0 stopped doing ftCommon_InitGrab, PAD_STACK(4) was added, and the scale Vec3 local was wrapped in a padded struct.
ftCo_800BD39C in CaptureKirby reached 100% according to the bot; ftCo_800BD1DC also reached 100% after the inline and stack-layout changes.
ftKb_SpecialNYs.c swapped FORCE_PAD_STACK usage for explicit u8 _pad[8] plus PAD_STACK(82) or PAD_STACK(0x54), finishing ftKb_YsSpecialNCapture2_0_Anim and ftKb_YsSpecialAirN2_1_Anim.

Several stage files added named /Gr*.dat strings and wrapper structs such as grBb_StageData, grKg_StageData, grMc_StageData, grIm_StageData, grHr_StageData, grOp_StageData, and grPu_StageData so StageData is contiguous with duplicated report/filename strings.

Stage GObj creation functions replaced direct string literals or __FILE__ in OSReport calls with explicit char arrays or pointer arithmetic into known data addresses, e.g. the repeated "%s:%d: couldn t get gobj(id=%d)\n" format.
grKongo_801D5340 now indexes grKg_803E1704[gobj_id] instead of treating grKg_803E16E0 as callbacks, and its static header now exposes correctly typed extern arrays.

grVenom_80205AD4 and grVenom_80206874 were substantially reshaped with explicit gotos, reordered zero initialization, pointer reloads after stores, and grCorneria_GroundVars2 casts for bitfield clearing; the bot showed both rising to roughly 99% but not full matches.
grMuteCity_801EFDF8 changed literal OSReport/HSD_ASSERT-style checks into explicit OSReport string-offset calls and __assert(filename, line, "0") calls using grMc_803E3434.
decomp-dev reported overall GALE01 gains: matched code 70.01% (+0.11%, +4304 bytes), linked code 42.35% (+0.05%, +2116 bytes), matched data 41.28% (+72 bytes), and linked data 35.00% (+88 bytes).
smart_moves: The PR linked the fighter units only after fixing both code shape and data ownership, avoiding a false code-only match that would still leave .sdata misplaced.
Modeling StageData plus trailing report/filename strings as wrapper structs preserved contiguous data layout while keeping the base StageData fields readable.
Using explicit local @ symbols in symbols.txt made anonymous compiler-emitted literals local instead of pretending they were global ftCo_/lbl_ objects.

The stage OSReport fixes intentionally referenced existing data addresses, sometimes through `(char*) callbacks + offset`, which is ugly but effective for matching loads of pooled or adjacent strings.
The grKongo header fix replaced an imprecise static pointer declaration with exact extern array types and lengths, making the data materialization reusable and less misleading.

The Venom work isolated non-structured-control-flow tactics behind labels like venom_80206874_spawn/link/default/done, documenting that the remaining gap is register allocation rather than algorithm discovery.
Stack layout fixes were localized: CaptureKirby used PAD_STACK and a padded local struct, while ftKb_SpecialNYs used explicit u8 padding plus PAD_STACK rather than broad rewrites.
decomp_lessons: When a unit is close to matching but still non-linkable, inspect splits.txt and symbols.txt; wrong .sdata ownership can keep unrelated neighboring files from matching.

Repeated strings such as OSReport formats may be physically adjacent to StageData rather than ordinary rodata literals; wrapping the struct with trailing char arrays can improve both data and code matching.
__FILE__ and string literals are not always acceptable substitutes for the original address; explicit filename char arrays and pointer-offset references may be needed.
For local compiler literals, prefer local @N symbols with `scope:local` in symbols.txt over invented global names unless there is evidence the symbol is global.
Small stack differences can be solved with targeted PAD_STACK, explicit dummy arrays, or padded local structs, especially around Vec3 locals passed to HSD_JObjSetScale.
Inlining boundaries matter: moving ftCommon_InitGrab out of inlineA0 in CaptureKirby changed the generated code shape enough to help match.
Use exact data declarations in static headers once arrays are identified; placeholder pointers can obscure callback indexing and produce bad codegen.
For near-matching control flow, explicit gotos can reproduce compiler branch layout more accurately than structured if/else, but should be paired with comments or meaningful label names.
Bitfield or union stores may need casts to a sibling ground-vars struct or explicit `*(s32*)` stores to force the same load/store width.
naming_conventions: Stage data symbols use the stage prefix plus address style, e.g. grBb_803E2D20, grKg_803E1800, grMc_803E33DC, grIm_803E4800.
Wrapper structs follow `grXX_StageData` and contain `StageData stage_data` followed by named trailing arrays such as `report_format`, `filename`, `block_num_msg`, `coll_jobj`, and `upper_msg`.
Known stage data filenames were pulled into named `char grXX_...[] = "/GrXX.dat"` or similar arrays instead of anonymous string literals.
Local compiler-generated data in config/GALE01/symbols.txt was renamed to `@NNN` with `scope:local`; the diff also added a local label named `...data.0` at 0x803BD0B0.
Internal castle callback tables became `static const`, matching local linkage instead of exported const objects.
grkongo.static.h now declares exact extern arrays, `S16Vec3 grKg_803E16E0[6]` and `StageCallbacks grKg_803E1704[12]`, rather than a generic static pointer.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Split .sdata ranges in splits.txt before marking objects Matching so local literals are assigned to the unit that actually owns them.
Use local @ symbols for floats, doubles, strings, and jump tables when the compiler emits local data rather than global labels.
Replace direct OSReport literals with explicit data symbols or address arithmetic into nearby arrays when the original binary loads strings from pooled/adjacent .data.
Create wrapper structs around StageData to include trailing format strings and filenames at the correct offsets without losing field readability.
Use explicit u8 padding arrays and PAD_STACK counts to force stack frame shape; examples include `u8 _pad[8]` plus `PAD_STACK(82)` and `PAD_STACK(0x54)` in ftKb_SpecialNYs.
For Vec3 locals, a padded anonymous struct can force the desired stack offset before passing `&scale` to HSD_JObjSetScale.
Reuse an input Vec3 pointer as temporary storage instead of introducing an extra local when that better matches register allocation, as in CaptureKirby inlineB1.
Reorder local declarations and initializations to influence register assignment, including the small itdraw declaration reorder and the early `zero = 0` in grVenom.
Use gotos to mirror original branch layout for grVenom type dispatch, then consolidate common tail code at a done label.
Use explicit casts such as `*(s32*)` and `((struct grCorneria_GroundVars2*) &gp->gv.venom)->xC4.flags.b0 = false` to control store width and bitfield access pattern.
Switch HSD_ASSERT macro use to explicit `__assert(filename, line, "0")` when macro expansion does not match the original callsite.
review_feedback: No human review comments or review records were present in the slice.
The PR body only says `Continuation of #2572`, so the detailed rationale must be inferred from the diff and bot metrics.

decomp-dev bot reported 18 new matches, including ftCo_800BD39C, grKongo_801D5340, grMuteCity_801EFD0C, grOldKongo_8020F52C, two ftKb_SpecialNYs animation functions, and several stage GObj creation functions.
The bot also reported 22 improvements in unmatched items, notably grKongo .data +425 bytes, grVenom_80206874 +130 bytes, and grVenom_80205AD4 +48 bytes.
The bot listed one zero-byte unmatched regression for grBigBlue_801E6364, while the overall report was strongly net-positive.
searchable_terms: More dups and a ft link
follow_up_queries: Inspect PR #2572 for the earlier part of the `dups` series.
git grep "couldn t get gobj" src/melee/gr
Check config/GALE01/splits.txt around ftCo_CaptureKirby.c, ftCo_CaptureWaitKirby.c, and ftCo_ThrownKirby.c.
Search symbols.txt for @218, @246, @253, and ftCo_804D8BA8 to review local literal ownership.
